message:
chore(beads): file syscall-surface fuzzing campaign (bd-jy4qu + 5 children)

Applied /testing-fuzzing discovery + scoring to identify five
high-leverage fuzz targets missing from crates/frankenlibc-fuzz:

- bd-o4yda: open/openat path+flags+mode validation
- bd-sttk9: read/write/pread/pwrite buffer+iovec validation
- bd-anoe7: pathname encoding (NUL/UTF-8/long/traversal/symlinks)
- bd-drs1c: printf/snprintf adversarial format strings (CVE-class)
- bd-l0ykk: env var handling (getenv/setenv/putenv/unsetenv,
  LD_PRELOAD/AT_SECURE)

Parent EPIC bd-jy4qu tracks the campaign; each child carries a
concrete input structure, invariant list, expected bug classes,
and a fault-sensitivity / independence / cost score (Hard Rule 1
from the /testing-fuzzing skill).

The current fuzz infrastructure unblock (bd-0ubkr, 3b74c87)
combined with the four fuzz-harvested fixes in the last session
(bd-m40be fnmatch bit-swap, bd-64uch fnmatch unterminated bracket,
bd-7rxtm strftime i32 overflows, bd-5koo6 difftime i64 overflow)
predict ~10 additional bugs from implementing these five harnesses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
